description: Understanding disability as social construction, not medical pathology. The social model: disability is not in the body — it is in the environment that fails to accommodate the body. Universal design, crip theory, disability justice.

dome_relevance.1: Legal — ADA, disability rights, guardianship reform
dome_relevance.2: Systems — accessibility of every system, form, portal
dome_relevance.4: Health — disability as identity, not pathology
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — self-determination, supported decision-making vs guardianship

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — accessible by design, not by retrofit
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — disability policy as design policy
